packets.name: packet-a
packets.scope: These 60 checked certificates classify exactly 20 cyclic two-orbit families around one published K43 seed at minimum monochromatic-K5 burden two. They do not cover arbitrary gluing configurations or the full graph space.
semantic_replay:
make -C tools/drat-trim drat-trim && python3 - <<'PY'
import json, subprocess
from pathlib import Path
r=json.loads(Path('artifacts/two_orbit_certified_sweep_report.json').read_text())
for family in r['families']:
    for cert in family['certificates'].values():
        subprocess.run(['tools/drat-trim/drat-trim', cert['cnf']['path'], cert['proof']['path']], check=True)
print('verified', sum(len(f['certificates']) for f in r['families']), 'DRAT certificates')
PY
packets.title: Packet A: generalized-gluing alignment

packets.name: packet-b
packets.scope: This packet authenticates the supplied corpus and reports only the independently validated 656-host embedding census recorded in its manifest. It does not prove that the supplied corpus is complete or classify all Ramsey(5,5,42) graphs.
packets.title: Packet B: CMU SRG alignment

packets.name: packet-c
packets.scope: This packet demonstrates a retained CNF/proof/checker-log/hash-manifest convention and corpus authentication. It does not claim to reproduce BigCompute's 656/656 extension run.
semantic_replay: replay=$(mktemp) && trap 'rm -f "$replay"' EXIT && python3 scripts/run_corpus_gate.py checkers/checker_a.py checkers/checker_b.c sources/r55_42some.g6 "$replay"
# Replay Packet A's 60 DRAT checks using the Packet A hook above.
packets.title: Packet C: BigCompute reproduction alignment
